source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Vector

Vector builds mobile and web technology that makes freight move faster. Our platform is used by warehouses, distribution centers, and carriers to streamline check-ins, appointments, dock scheduling, and driver workflows.



About the Role

You'll join our web application team, working alongside frontend engineers, designers, and product managers to build and improve the features our customers rely on every day. Our webapp is built with React and TypeScript.



What You’ll Do

 - Help architect our web application

 - Work with designers to build UI components

 - Write tests
   
   

What We’re Looking For

 - Demonstrated professional engineering experience building complex, commercially available software applications

 - Fluency with TypeScript/JavaScript, CSS, and HTML5

 - Proficiency with Javascript frameworks such as React and Angular

 - Strong understanding, building and debugging of scalable, high performance web applications built with reusability and componentization in mind

 - Experience building front end components with React using modular architecture

 - Knowledge of state management frameworks, such as Redux or Flux

 - Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science or a related field preferred. You should be able to demonstrate superior understanding of CS fundamentals



Benefits

 - Employer-sponsored medical, dental, vision, 401K, life & disability insurance

 - Flexible paid time off and observed federal holidays

 - Stock options

 - Parental leave of absence

 - Flat org structure, offering startup growth and learning paths



Salary Range

$160,000 - $180,000 USD

Individual pay depends on various factors, such as region, role level, relevant experience and skills. Pay ranges are reviewed and updated typically each year. Offers are made within the pay range applicable at the time.

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Vector

Vector builds mobile and web technology that makes freight move faster. Our platform is used by warehouses, distribution centers, and carriers to streamline check-ins, appointments, dock scheduling, and driver workflows.



About the Role

As a Product Manager at Vector, you’ll be responsible for building the tools that will modernize the logistics industry. Product Management at Vector sits at the intersection of software development, design, and business strategy. We shape high-level product goals but also get our hands dirty. On an average day, you might conduct an in-depth interview with a customer stakeholder, pitch a concept to the team, create a launch plan with marketing, conduct user research, prototype a new feature, iterate on a PRD, analyze usage data, and pair with an engineer on implementation. We have ultimate responsibility for understanding our users’ needs, influencing business strategy, feature specification, and working with solutions and engineering teams to develop our software for successful delivery to our customers.



What You'll Do

 - Own the roadmap for deploying and administering Vector at enterprise scale

 - As an embedded partner within our Solutions Engineering organization, identify untapped strategies and efficiencies to streamline and accelerate development cycles

 - Prioritize potential projects through demonstrating their alignment with our business strategy and the value to our current or future users

 - Communicate that prioritization to other stakeholders in the company and drive consensus on the path forward

 - Work closely with engineering, design, customer success and other team members on business cases, product requirements, development, launch, and adoption

 - Understand Vector’s strategic position and deliver innovative products that align with this strategy

 - Work directly with our clients to unearth pain points and understand their most pressing needs

 - Communicate with the rest of the company the how and why for features



What We're Looking For

 - Previous experience in enterprise / B2B SaaS product management

 - A track record of execution in project management showing impact in driving projects forward within an organization

 - Entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to build strong data-driven arguments, prioritize with business strategy in mind, and evangelize projects to build consensus within an organization

 - Outstanding communication and interpersonal skills - interface with our clients as well as internal stakeholders: solutions engineers, deployments, client success, sales and marketing

 - Thrive in a startup environment and in ambiguity

 - Active listener with exceptional customer focus

 - High integrity, able to build trust and collaborate through differing opinions



Benefits

 - Employer-sponsored medical, dental, vision, 401K, life & disability insurance

 - Flexible paid time off and observed federal holidays

 - Stock options

 - Parental leave of absence

 - Competitive stipend

 - Flat org structure, offering startup growth and learning paths



Salary Range

$175,000 - $200,000 USD

Individual pay depends on various factors, such as region, role level, relevant experience and skills. Pay ranges are reviewed and updated typically each year. Offers are made within the pay range applicable at the time.

source_jobs.title: Implementation Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Vector

Vector builds mobile and web technology that makes freight move faster. Our platform is used by warehouses, distribution centers, and carriers to streamline check-ins, appointments, dock scheduling, and driver workflows.



About the Role

As a ‘doer’, you will ultimately be responsible for the crucial, tactical fundamentals of a software application deployment, and the success of the day to day dynamics between the Vector team and our customer. A successful candidate will demonstrate strengths in interpersonal skills and communication, be highly organized and be an ace at problem resolution. They will seek out and thrive on customer feedback in order to continuously improve the project, team, and customer experience overall. Our ideal candidate will be highly attuned to the needs of various stakeholders, gain a deep understanding of the customer and their business, as well as become well versed in how our customers achieve great outcomes with Vector



What You'll Do

 - Deliver excellence in Operational Engagement and day to day management on key facility accounts

 - Provide end user support, training, and change management on software app deployments

 - Provide an in person Vector presence for both go-lives and ongoing support as part of a comprehensive Customer Success strategy

 - Provide updates and status reports, both internally and customer facing regularly to ensure real time visibility and proper decision-making

 - Provide play by play updates from the field to the teams, identifying, escalating and triaging issues as they arise

 - Become Vector’s internal subject matter expert on the comprehensive operational needs of our customers



What We're Looking For

 - Experience / ability as a core customer facing member deploying SaaS / enterprise software on key accounts or initiatives

 - Demonstrated ability to run operational efforts and tactical meetings with minimal oversight

 - Comfortable with driving the process, raising flags or triaging when appropriate

 - Proven ability to onboard multiple waves of facility deployments and work with Deployment Strategists to define the roadmap for continuous waves of future deployments

 - Exceptional rapport building skills with stakeholders at all levels, gaining continuous positive customer feedback on interpersonal and industry skills

 - Able to to travel up to 50% to customer locations

 - Drive for results and a 'whatever it takes' attitude

 - Ability to escalate issues using sound judgment and critical thinking abilities

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Highly organized and detail oriented



Benefits

 - Employer-sponsored medical, dental, vision, 401K, life & disability insurance

 - Flexible paid time off and observed federal holidays

 - Stock options

 - Parental leave of absence

 - Competitive stipend

 - Flat org structure, offering startup growth and learning paths



Salary Range

$110,000 - $130,000 USD

Your compensation may be based on several factors, including education, work experience, and region.

source_jobs.title: Software Solutions Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Vector

Vector builds mobile and web technology that makes freight move faster. Our platform is used by warehouses, distribution centers, and carriers to streamline check-ins, appointments, dock scheduling, and driver workflows.



About the Role

Our enterprise implementations often call for digitizing paperwork processes within the client’s mobile app. Our Solutions Engineers will possess the technical aptitude to build these digital workflow schemas through Vector’s internal tools. Our ideal candidate has a passion for logistics and technology along with a history of providing a superior client experience. You’ll be expected to display a strong sense of self-reliance as well as the ability to collaborate with internal Vector teams such as Deployment Managers, Customer Success, and core Software Engineering. If this sounds like you, let’s chat!

You will work closely with Vector’s Software Engineers and Deployment Managers in a coordinated and collaborative customer deployment experience ensuring that Vector and our customers' technical integration is aligned. This approach ensures that all aspects of the customer deployment are completed so that the training, on-boarding and go-live components of the customer implementation are successful.



What You'll Do

 - Engage with our customer's business and technical resources to fully understand their project goals, objectives and success criteria as part of each project implementation engagement

 - Build workflows and automate tasks for customers leveraging a combination of scripting languages, JSON, and Vector’s internal tools

 - Setup and configure Vector according to customer's business and technical needs

 - Triage any deployment or integration issues quickly, leveraging resources from across the company as needed

 - Participate in and drive technical discovery sessions to deliver technical integration solutions for our customers

 - Hone your expertise on Vector deployments and be the point person to help guide product implementation feedback and learnings within Vector

 - Be a key player in coordinating project implementation plans, timelines, schedules and develop strategy around customer deliverables

 - Gather customer product feedback and communicate with product management and engineering to shape product roadmap development

 - Coordinate with internal Vector resources from Sales, Engineering, Product and Customer Success to ensure customer business and technical requirements are correctly understood and mapped to product capabilities and future roadmap investments

 - Be the voice of our customers’ technical needs and issues internally, across departments and work with product management to identify, drive and prioritize improvements in product offerings, processes, systems and tools



What We're Looking For

 - Previous experience in a software engineering, web development, client consulting, or technical implementation role

 - Demonstrated ability in implementing SaaS software applications and/or providing technical implementation services leveraging basic programming such as SQL and JavaScript

 - Genuine passion for delighting customers, and for building and maintaining strong relationships with a diverse set of internal and external stakeholders, from engineering, product, marketing, etc

 - Great communication skills - able to provide clear and concise guidance through emails, over the phone, or in person, with a casual and confident tone

 - Excellent organization, project management, time management, and communication skills

 - Quick learner, able to fully grasp technological detail as well as high-level business concepts

 - Able to gain consensus and get business and technical teams to work together toward timely issue resolution

 - A strong awareness of business processes and ability to translate business processes into enterprise application implementations

 - Effective team player dedicated to continuously improve upon our process of adv

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About Vector

Vector builds mobile and web technology that makes freight move faster. Our platform is used by warehouses, distribution centers, and carriers to streamline check-ins, appointments, dock scheduling, and driver workflows.



About the Role

As an Enterprise Account Executive at Vector, you’ll be at the forefront of transforming logistics and facility operations through our digital workflow solutions which include eBOL, Digital Check-In and Yard Management System. In this strategic role, you’ll lead the full sales cycle—developing pipeline, creating demand, and guiding enterprise customers through complex buying decisions. You’ll work closely with Solutions Architects, SDRs, and executive leadership in a team-based selling motion to align our offering to customer pain points, build executive trust, and deliver a compelling ROI-backed business case.

We’re looking for a commercially driven, consultative seller with experience navigating large organizations, building multi-threaded relationships, and influencing both operational and financial stakeholders.



What You'll Do

 - Own a book of business that includes net new logos and upsell with existing customers

 - Execute account strategies to identify opportunities aligned with customer initiatives

 - Partner with Solutions Architects to diagnose customer challenges and demonstrate tailored solutions to the buyers needs

 - Quarterback a deal team support from SDR(s), solution architects, executives, and product teams

 - Drive executive alignment across business and IT stakeholders to secure buy-in and budget

 - Partner with Pre-Sales to deliver compelling business cases and ROI models in collaboration with customer finance teams

 - Forecast opportunities accurately and manage deals through long enterprise sales cycles

 - Travel up to 50% to support in-person discovery, workshops, executive meetings, and onsite presentations

 - Attend 3-6 industry conferences per year as part of continual pipeline development

 - Owner the relationship with one industry association or council; foster relationships, cross-selling, and being a voice for Vector’s brand in the market



What We're Looking For

 - 5-10 years of Sales experience with a passion for quarterbacking a deal team

 - 3+ years of enterprise SaaS sales experience with a track record of quota attainment

 - Experience navigating enterprise organizations and building relationships across operational and executive levels

 - Strong understanding of solution selling, consultative sales approaches, and value-based selling

 - Comfortable running ROI analyses and business case development with procurement and finance stakeholders

 - Collaborative mindset with the ability to work in a team-selling environment

 - Excellent verbal, written, and presentation skills



Benefits

 - Employer-sponsored medical, dental, vision, 401K, life & disability insurance

 - Flexible paid time off and observed federal holidays

 - Stock options

 - Remote work

 - Parental leave of absence

 - Competitive stipend

 - One-time home office stipend

 - Flat org structure, offering startup growth and learning paths



Salary Range

$125,000 - $175,000 USD

A commission-based plan will also be a part of employee compensation

Individual pay depends on various factors, such as region, role level, relevant experience and skills. Pay ranges are reviewed and typically updated each year. Offers are made within the pay range applicable at the time.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Vector

Vector builds mobile and web technology that makes freight move faster. Our platform is used by warehouses, distribution centers, and carriers to streamline check-ins, appointments, dock scheduling, and driver workflows.



About the Role

In your role as a Senior Software Engineer you will work with others to design, develop, and implement any changes, bug fixes, and new features to our web application used by our customers such as FedEx, Home Depot, Coca Cola, Sprouts, Clorox, Kraft Heinz and many others.

You'll be working alongside team members in engineering, design and product to build and improve the features our customers rely on every day. Our web app is built with React and TypeScript.



What You’ll Do

 - Work on a lean, experienced engineering team and participate in all aspects of the development process

 - Actively participate in architecture discussions

 - Develop new features and modify existing features

 - Conduct code reviews and collaborate with other engineers

 - Continuously look for ways to improve our code base and evaluate new technologies
   
   

What We’re Looking For

 - Demonstrated professional engineering experience building complex, commercially shipped software products

 - Strong understanding, building and debugging of scalable, high performance web applications built with reusability and componentization in mind

 - Proficiency with Javascript frameworks such as React and Angular

 - Advanced level knowledge of TypeScript/JavaScript, CSS, and HTML5

 - Experience building front end components with React using modular architecture

 - Knowledge of state management frameworks, such as Redux or Flux

 - Strong critical reasoning and problem solving abilities

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills

 - Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science or a related field preferred You should be able to demonstrate superior understanding of CS fundamentals



Benefits

 - Employer-sponsored medical, dental, vision, 401K, life & disability insurance

 - Flexible paid time off and observed federal holidays

 - Stock options

 - Parental leave of absence

 - Flat org structure, offering startup growth and learning paths



Salary Range

$180,000 - $200,000 USD

Individual pay depends on various factors, such as region, role level, relevant experience and skills. Pay ranges are reviewed and updated typically each year. Offers are made within the pay range applicable at the time.

notes: Vector builds mobile and web technology for freight logistics, with a strong focus on engineering and product development.
